description: Schema for the NN simulation input file. Configures a neural-network-based solver for phase-field fracture: the network architecture, training loop, displacement loading schedule, and output options. References a parent input file for the physical problem definition (material, mesh, BCs, etc.).

displacement_parameters.description: Two-stage displacement loading schedule. The simulation first ramps through a 'coarse' phase (large increments, pre-crack regime) then switches to a 'fine' phase (small increments, around crack initiation and propagation) to balance speed with resolution.

coarse_end.description: Displacement value at which the coarse phase ends and the fine phase begins. Should be chosen just before the expected onset of damage.

end.description: Final displacement value at the end of the fine phase — the last load step of the simulation.

increments_coarse.description: Number of equal displacement increments in the coarse phase (from start to coarse_end). Fewer increments means larger jumps and faster traversal of the elastic regime.

increments_fine.description: Number of equal displacement increments in the fine phase (from coarse_end to end). More increments give better resolution of crack initiation and propagation.

loading_angle.description: Angle (in degrees, 0–90) at which the displacement is applied relative to the horizontal axis. 0 is pure horizontal (mode-II shear), 90 is pure vertical (mode-I tension).

start.description: Displacement value at the first load step. Typically 0 for loading from an undeformed state.

model_energy_scale.description: How the network's energy output is scaled. 'linear' uses the raw output directly. 'log' transforms the output to the log scale.

network.description: Architecture and initialisation settings for the neural network.

activation.description: Configuration of the activation function applied after each hidden layer.

initial_coefficient.description: Starting value of the learnable scaling coefficient applied to the activation. Only meaningful when trainable is true; otherwise this fixed value is used throughout.

trainable.description: If true, the activation scaling coefficient is treated as a learnable parameter and updated during training.

trainable_global.description: Controls whether the trainable coefficient is shared across all layers (true) or each layer gets its own independent coefficient (false). Only relevant when trainable is true.

hidden_count.description: Number of total hidden layers between the input and output layers.

hidden_size.description: Number of neurons in each hidden layer (all hidden layers use the same width).

input_size.description: Dimension of the network input. 2 for 2D problems.

output_size.description: Dimension of the network output. 3 for 2D problems.

rff.description: Random Fourier Features — a fixed nonlinear input embedding that helps the network learn high-frequency content (fine spatial detail) without spectral bias.

enabled.description: If true, the raw input coordinates are mapped through a random Fourier feature layer before entering the first hidden layer.

features.description: Number of random Fourier basis functions. More features capture higher frequencies but increase the effective input dimension.

scale.description: Standard deviation of the random frequency matrix.

type.description: Network architecture variant. Determines the forward-pass structure beyond the basic layer stack.

weight_initialisation_type.description: Scheme for initialising weight matrices. Affects early training dynamics; should be matched to the chosen activation function.

output_parameters.description: Controls which result files and diagnostics are produced during and after training.

log_parameters.description: Fine-grained control over what gets recorded in the training log file.

calculate_sparsity.description: If true, the fraction of near-zero activations is computed and logged at each reporting step. Useful for diagnosing dead neurons or overly sparse representations.

output_activation_coefficients.description: If true, the current values of the learnable activation scaling coefficients are included in the log at each reporting step. Only meaningful when activation.trainable is true.

pvd_gif_parameters.description: Post-processing step that renders the saved PVD files into an animated GIF for quick visual inspection.

axis_limits.description: Fixed axis bounds for every frame ([x/y_min, x/y_max]).

distortion_scaling.description: Multiplier applied to the displacement field before rendering. Values greater than 1 exaggerate deformations for visibility; 1.0 shows true scale.

enabled.description: If true, a GIF is generated from the PVD time series after the simulation finishes.

fps.description: Playback speed of the output GIF in frames per second.

include_every_n.description: Frame-skipping factor. A value of 1 includes every timestep; 2 includes every second timestep, etc. Useful for keeping file size down on long runs.

mesh_outline.description: If true, element edges are drawn over the contour plot so the mesh topology is visible.

target_fields.description: Names of the nodal/elemental fields to visualise (e.g. 'displacement', 'phasefield'). One GIF is produced per field.

save_logs.description: If true, training diagnostics (loss history, timing) are written to a log file.

save_pvd.description: If true, ParaView-compatible PVD/VTU files are written for field visualisation. The frequency is controlled by training_snapshots.

stream_outputs.description: If true, post-processed quantities of interest are flushed to files incrementally during the run, rather than only at the end. Useful for long simulations or when memory is limited.

timestamp.description: If true, output directories and filenames include a timestamp to avoid overwriting results from previous runs.

training_snapshots.description: Controls periodic PVD snapshots of the predicted fields during training, so you can watch the solution evolve as the network learns.

enabled.description: If true, PVD snapshots are saved periodically during training at the interval set by frequency.

frequency.description: Save a PVD snapshot every N training epochs. Lower values give finer-grained progress tracking but produce more files.

parent_input_file.description: Path to another input JSON that this file inherits from.

phasefield.description: NN-specific overrides and additions for the phase-field model, beyond what the parent file defines. Controls the irreversibility constraint and how it is enforced numerically.

constraint.description: How the phase-field irreversibility condition (damage can only increase) is enforced within the neural network formulation.

numerical_coefficient.description: Scaling coefficient for the numerical penalty/barrier that enforces irreversibility. Larger values enforce the constraint more strictly but can destabilise training.

numerical_offset.description: Offset added to the constraint to avoid exact-zero divisions or log(0) in the barrier function.

numerical_support.description: Width of the phase-field contraint function's support.

type.description: Enforcement strategy. 'numerical' uses a piecewise sigmoid. 'analytical' uses a pure sigmoid.

irreversibility_tolerance.description: Factor for the phase field irreversibility energy term.

pretraining_parameters.description: Optional pretraining phase that trains the network on a simpler objective (i.e. on a smaller mesh) before the main phase-field training begins. Provides a better starting point for the main optimisation.

enabled.description: If true, the pretraining phase runs before the main training loop. If false, pretraining is skipped entirely.

mesh.description: Mesh used for evaluating the pretraining loss. Can differ from the main simulation mesh (e.g. a coarser mesh to speed up pretraining).

filename.description: Path to the pretraining mesh file.

number_of_integration_points.description: Number of Gauss quadrature points per element for the pretraining mesh.

type.description: Element topology for the pretraining mesh. Must be one of the supported types.

optimiser.description: Ordered list of optimiser configurations to use during pretraining. Multiple entries allow chaining (e.g. Adam then L-BFGS).

reset_activation_coefficient.description: If true, any trainable activation coefficients learned during pretraining are reset to their initial values before the main training begins. Prevents the pretraining solution from biasing the activation shape.

seed.description: Global random seed for reproducibility. Controls weight initialisation, random Fourier features, and any other stochastic operations. Use different seeds to run independent replicates.

training_parameters.description: Configuration of the main training loop: optimiser(s), loss composition, early stopping, and logging frequency.

early_stopping.description: Terminates training when the loss stops improving, preventing wasted compute and potential overfitting.

absolute_threshold.description: Minimum absolute improvement in loss required to count as progress. Used when mode is 'absolute'.

enabled.description: If true, training may terminate before all epochs are exhausted if the loss stagnates.

mode.description: How improvement is measured. 'absolute' compares the raw loss decrease against absolute_threshold. 'relative' compares the fractional decrease against relative_threshold.

patience.description: Number of consecutive epochs without qualifying improvement before training is stopped. Higher patience tolerates longer plateaus.

pretraining_stop.description: If true, the same early stopping logic also applies during the pretraining phase. If false, pretraining always runs to completion.

relative_threshold.description: Minimum fractional improvement (e.g. 0.001 = 0.1%) required to count as progress. Used when mode is 'relative'.

loss_reporting_frequency.description: Print/log the current loss value every N epochs. Lower values give more dense loss reporting in the log.

loss_terms.description: Toggles for the individual components of the total loss function. At least the elastic energy should normally be enabled.

energy_elastic.description: If true, the elastic strain energy contribution is included in the loss. This is the core mechanical driving term.

energy_irreversibility.description: If true, a penalty term enforcing crack irreversibility (damage cannot heal) is added to the loss. Works alongside the phasefield.constraint settings.

energy_phasefield.description: If true, the phase-field crack surface energy (regularised Griffith energy) is included. Required for crack propagation simulations.

weight_decay.description: If true, an L2 penalty on network weights is added to the loss. Strength is controlled by training_parameters.weight_decay.

optimiser.description: Ordered list of optimiser configurations for the main training loop. Multiple entries allow chaining (e.g. Adam for initial convergence then L-BFGS for fine polishing).

weight_decay.description: L2 regularisation coefficient. Penalises large weights to improve generalisation and conditioning. Only active when loss_terms.weight_decay is true. Typical value 1e-5.

transfer_learning.description: Warm-starts the network from a previously trained model (e.g. from a nearby load step or a coarser mesh). Speeds up convergence by reusing learned features rather than training from scratch.

enabled.description: If true, the network weights (and optionally the optimiser state) are loaded from a previous checkpoint before training begins.

n_transferable_states.description: Number of previous optimiser state snapshots to carry over (e.g. momentum buffers in Adam). Only relevant when transfer_optimiser is true.

transfer_optimiser.description: If true, the optimiser's internal state (momentum, variance estimates, etc.) is also loaded from the checkpoint, not just the network weights. Preserves training dynamics across transfers.
